members.name: _GetExtensions
members.summary: Return supported file extensions for this scene importer.

members.name: _GetImportOptions
members.summary: Override to add general import options. These will appear in the main import dock on the editor. Add options via [method add_import_option] and [method add_import_option_advanced]. [b]Note:[/b] All [EditorSceneFormatImporter] and [EditorScenePostImportPlugin] instances will add options for all files. It is good practice to check the file extension when [param path] is non-empty. When the user is editing project settings, [param path] will be empty. It is recommended to add all options when [param path] is empty to allow the user to customize Import Defaults.

members.name: _GetOptionVisibility
members.signature: public Variant _GetOptionVisibility(String path, bool forAnimation, String option)
members.summary: Should return [code]true[/code] to show the given option, [code]false[/code] to hide the given option, or [code]null[/code] to ignore.

members.name: _ImportScene
members.signature: public Object _ImportScene(String path, int flags, Dictionary options)
members.summary: Perform the bulk of the scene import logic here, for example using [GLTFDocument] or [FBXDocument].

members.name: AddImportOption
members.signature: public void AddImportOption(String name, Variant value)
members.summary: Add a specific import option (name and default value only). This function can only be called from [method _get_import_options].

members.name: AddImportOptionAdvanced
members.signature: public void AddImportOptionAdvanced(int type, String name, Variant defaultValue, int hint, String hintString, int usageFlags)
members.summary: Add a specific import option. This function can only be called from [method _get_import_options].

values.name: IMPORT_ANIMATION
values.summary: Import animations from the 3D scene. When importing a scene as an [AnimationLibrary], this flag is always enabled.

values.name: IMPORT_DISCARD_MESHES_AND_MATERIALS
values.summary: Ignore meshes and materials on import. When importing a scene as an [AnimationLibrary], this flag is always enabled.

values.name: IMPORT_FAIL_ON_MISSING_DEPENDENCIES
values.summary: Unused flag (this has no effect when enabled).

values.name: IMPORT_FORCE_DISABLE_MESH_COMPRESSION
values.summary: If [code]true[/code], mesh compression will not be used. Consider enabling if you notice blocky artifacts in your mesh normals or UVs, or if you have meshes that are larger than a few thousand meters in each direction.

values.name: IMPORT_GENERATE_TANGENT_ARRAYS
values.summary: If [code]true[/code], generate vertex tangents using [url=http://www.mikktspace.com/]Mikktspace[/url] if the input meshes don't have tangent data. When possible, it's recommended to let the 3D modeling software generate tangents on export instead of relying on this option. Tangents are required for correct display of normal and height maps, along with any material/shader features that require tangents. If you don't need material features that require tangents, disabling this can reduce output file size and speed up importing if the source 3D file doesn't contain tangents.

values.name: IMPORT_SCENE
values.summary: Unused flag (this has no effect when enabled).

values.name: IMPORT_USE_NAMED_SKIN_BINDS
values.summary: If checked, use named [Skin]s for animation. The [MeshInstance3D] node contains 3 properties of relevance here: a skeleton [NodePath] pointing to the [Skeleton3D] node (usually [code]..[/code]), a mesh, and a skin: - The [Skeleton3D] node contains a list of bones with names, their pose and rest, a name, and a parent bone. - The mesh is all of the raw vertex data needed to display a mesh. In terms of the mesh, it knows how vertices are weight-painted and uses some internal numbering often imported from 3D modeling software. - The skin contains the information necessary to bind this mesh onto this Skeleton3D. For each of the internal bone IDs chosen by the 3D modeling software, it contains two things. Firstly, a matrix known as the Bind Pose Matrix, Inverse Bind Matrix, or IBM for short. Secondly, the [Skin] contains each bone's name (if this flag is enabled), or the bone's index within the [Skeleton3D] list (if this flag is disabled). Together, this information is enough to tell Godot how to use the bone poses in the [Skeleton3D] node to render the mesh from each [MeshInstance3D]. Note that each [MeshInstance3D] may share binds, as is common in models exported from Blender, or each [MeshInstance3D] may use a separate [Skin] object, as is common in models exported from other tools such as Maya.

constants.name: IMPORT_SCENE
constants.summary: Unused flag (this has no effect when enabled).

If you don't need material features that require tangents, disabling this can reduce output file size and speed up importing if the source 3D file doesn't contain tangents.

constants.summary: If checked, use named [Skin]s for animation. For each of the internal bone IDs chosen by the 3D modeling software, it contains two things. Firstly, a matrix known as the Bind Pose Matrix, Inverse Bind Matrix, or IBM for short.
